purpose: Timing side-channel budget contract for representative pointer, allocator, string, stdio, and pthread paths across strict and hardened modes. Strict and hardened intentionally differ in validation depth and repair behavior; this manifest pins an upper bound on the *p99 timing delta* between the two modes so an input class that triggers a deeper validation path cannot leak that fact through an unbounded timing channel. NO production timing behavior changes are required by this bead — the manifest is the contract any future hardening/optimization bead must respect.

policy.amplification_rationale: If the worst-input-class p99 delta exceeds 3x the typical-input p99 delta on the same path, treat it as suspicious amplification: a small subset of inputs is consuming disproportionate hardened-mode work and may leak class membership via timing.

paths.rationale: Pointer validation is the canonical hot-path that distinguishes the two modes; hardened performs canary + bounds re-checks that strict can skip.
paths.intentional_divergence_rationale: Hardened mode runs the full bounds + canary re-check; strict mode trusts a recent cache hit. The 200ns cap matches the hardened budget.

paths.rationale: Fast-path allocations bypass hardened tracking; the delta is bounded so an attacker cannot fingerprint allocator class by timing.
paths.intentional_divergence_rationale: Hardened mode tracks allocation provenance for canary checks; strict skips.

paths.rationale: Both modes must run constant-time memcmp on equal-length inputs; deltas above 50ns indicate the hardened path took a non-constant-time branch.
paths.intentional_divergence_rationale: memcmp is required to be effectively constant-time across modes for security workloads.

paths.rationale: Small reads (<= 4096 bytes) hit the FILE buffer hot path; deltas reflect lock geometry differences.
paths.intentional_divergence_rationale: Hardened mode acquires a per-FILE membrane lock around the buffer; strict can avoid it for non-shared FILEs.

paths.rationale: Uncontended mutex lock is the smallest synchronization primitive; the 100ns budget matches the strict<200ns hardened budget for hot paths.
paths.intentional_divergence_rationale: Mutex lock geometry must be timing-stable across modes for security workloads that use mutex acquisition as a fairness signal.

input_classes.rationale: Mainline well-formed input — the budget baseline.
Inputs that land exactly on a fast-path boundary (alignment, size class). Should not amplify the delta.
Inputs deliberately chosen to maximize hardened-mode work (e.g., crossing a magazine boundary, triggering a bloom miss). Amplification beyond amplification_threshold_ratio fails closed.

downstream_consumers.consumes: RCU/BRAVO read-mostly fast-path prototype must respect this budget — moving a hot path to a faster lock cannot widen the strict/hardened delta beyond the per-path cap.

regeneration_note: When a path's strict-vs-hardened semantic gap changes (e.g., hardened gains a new check), update allowed_p99_delta_budget_ns and intentional_divergence_rationale atomically with source_commit. Do NOT raise the cap silently — the harness checker fails closed when delta exceeds budget; raising the cap requires a security review note in this manifest's commit message.
